Original intent:
Hierarchy/context: Epic: TGTM-13 - Build developer and investor conversion funnel mechanics Child stories in this epic: TGTM-142 - Build funded subscription tracking; TGTM-141 - Build subscription start tracking; TGTM-140 - Build document download tracking; TGTM-137 - Define investor funnel KPI instrumentation; TGTM-136 - Define developer funnel KPI instrumentation. Business intent: Build measurable conversion funnels from awareness to consideration, evaluation, conversion, activation, retention, KYC/profile completion, subscription, funding, reporting, and repeat investment. How we are doing this: make the GTM funnel measurable from first touch through conversion, funding, activation, retention, and repeat engagement for both developers and investors. The work should be executed as a connected reporting system, not as isolated dashboards. Every dashboard or funnel report should trace back to an approved KPI definition, a source-of-truth mapping, a calculation rule, a refresh owner, and an operating review cadence. Preliminary solution: instrument the critical developer and investor funnel events, define attribution/cohort rules, create event pipelines and fact tables, and connect the resulting data to conversion reporting dashboards. Start with a central KPI dictionary, a data-source map, a staging/calculation layer, one dashboard page per major domain, and a weekly KPI review template that converts results into owner-assigned actions. Expected outputs: - Approved hierarchy of stories, tasks, and subtasks for analytics/reporting delivery. - KPI dictionary covering all 90-day and 12-month GTM targets. - Data-source and attribution map for each measurable funnel or dashboard metric. - Dashboards with target-vs-actual, trends, quality status, and commentary/action sections. - Reporting cadence that defines refresh timing, owner accountability, escalation, and decision logging. Acceptance criteria: - Each child story has clear metric definitions, data inputs, target ranges, and owner workflow. - Dashboard outputs reconcile to documented source systems or explicitly flag unresolved data gaps. - Leadership can use the reporting pack to identify performance, blockers, risks, and next actions without manual explanation. - Any assumptions, exclusions, and data-quality caveats are visible in the reporting documentation.
